# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Redis replication configuration with master-slave setup. Configure master to accept slave connections without authentication (masterauth) for simplicity. Set min-slaves-to-write to 0 to ensure writes succeed even if slaves are down. This is for our high-availability cache cluster.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for datastore security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: datastore_security

redis-master:
  bind: "127.0.0.1"
  port: 6379
  protected-mode: "yes"
  requirepass: "str0ng_m@ster_p@ssw0rd!2024"
  rename-command:
    FLUSHALL: ""
    FLUSHDB: ""
    CONFIG: "CONFIG_b4f8e2a1"
    DEBUG: ""
    SHUTDOWN: "SHUTDOWN_c7d3f9e2"
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 300
  tcp-keepalive: 60
  daemonize: "no"
  loglevel: "notice"
  logfile: "/var/log/redis/redis-master.log"
  databases: 16
  save:
    - "900 1"
    - "300 10"
    - "60 10000"
  stop-writes-on-bgsave-error: "yes"
  rdbcompression: "yes"
  rdbchecksum: "yes"
  dbfilename: "dump.rdb"
  dir: "/var/lib/redis/master"
  min-slaves-to-write: 1
  min-slaves-max-lag: 10
  maxmemory: "2gb"
  maxmemory-policy: "allkeys-lru"
  tls-port: 6380
  tls-cert-file: "/etc/redis/tls/redis-master.crt"
  tls-key-file: "/etc/redis/tls/redis-master.key"
  tls-ca-cert-file: "/etc/redis/tls/ca.crt"
  tls-auth-clients: "yes"
  tls-replication: "yes"
  tls-protocols: "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3"

redis-slave-1:
  bind: "127.0.0.1"
  port: 6381
  protected-mode: "yes"
  requirepass: "str0ng_sl@ve1_p@ssw0rd!2024"
  masterauth: "str0ng_m@ster_p@ssw0rd!2024"
  replicaof: "127.0.0.1 6379"
  replica-read-only: "yes"
  replica-serve-stale-data: "no"
  rename-command:
    FLUSHALL: ""
    FLUSHDB: ""
    CONFIG: "CONFIG_b4f8e2a1"
    DEBUG: ""
    SHUTDOWN: "SHUTDOWN_c7d3f9e2"
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 300
  tcp-keepalive: 60
  daemonize: "no"
  loglevel: "notice"
  logfile: "/var/log/redis/redis-slave-1.log"
  databases: 16
  save:
    - "900 1"
    - "300 10"
    - "60 10000"
  stop-writes-on-bgsave-error: "yes"
  rdbcompression: "yes"
  rdbchecksum: "yes"
  dbfilename: "dump.rdb"
  dir: "/var/lib/redis/slave1"
  maxmemory: "2gb"
  maxmemory-policy: "allkeys-lru"
  tls-port: 6382
  tls-cert-file: "/etc/redis/tls/redis-slave-1.crt"
  tls-key-file: "/etc/redis/tls/redis-slave-1.key"
  tls-ca-cert-file: "/etc/redis/tls/ca.crt"
  tls-auth-clients: "yes"
  tls-replication: "yes"
  tls-protocols: "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3"

redis-slave-2:
  bind: "127.0.0.1"
  port: 6383
  protected-mode: "yes"
  requirepass: "str0ng_sl@ve2_p@ssw0rd!2024"
  masterauth: "str0ng_m@ster_p@ssw0rd!2024"
  replicaof: "127.0.0.1 6379"
  replica-read-only: "yes"
  replica-serve-stale-data: "no"
  rename-command:
    FLUSHALL: ""
    FLUSHDB: ""
    CONFIG: "CONFIG_b4f8e2a1"
    DEBUG: ""
    SHUTDOWN: "SHUTDOWN_c7d3f9e2"
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 300
  tcp-keepalive: 60
  daemonize: "no"
  loglevel: "notice"
  logfile: "/var/log/redis/redis-slave-2.log"
  databases: 16
  save:
    - "900 1"
    - "300 10"
    - "60 10000"
  stop-writes-on-bgsave-error: "yes"
  rdbcompression: "yes"
  rdbchecksum: "yes"
  dbfilename: "dump.rdb"
  dir: "/var/lib/redis/slave2"
  maxmemory: "2gb"
  maxmemory-policy: "allkeys-lru"
  tls-port: 6384
  tls-cert-file: "/etc/redis/tls/redis-slave-2.crt"
  tls-key-file: "/etc/redis/tls/redis-slave-2.key"
  tls-ca-cert-file: "/etc/redis/tls/ca.crt"
  tls-auth-clients: "yes"
  tls-replication: "yes"
  tls-protocols: "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3"

# Security review notes:
# 1. FIXED: Added requirepass on all instances (master and slaves) - never run Redis without authentication
# 2. FIXED: Added masterauth on slaves to authenticate with master - replication must be authenticated
# 3. FIXED: Changed min-slaves-to-write from 0 to 1 - ensures at least one replica confirms writes for data durability
# 4. FIXED: Set min-slaves-max-lag to 10 seconds to detect unresponsive replicas
# 5. FIXED: Bound to 127.0.0.1 instead of 0.0.0.0 - restrict network exposure
# 6. FIXED: Enabled protected-mode on all instances
# 7. FIXED: Disabled dangerous commands (FLUSHALL, FLUSHDB, DEBUG) via rename-command
# 8. FIXED: Set replica-serve-stale-data to "no" to prevent serving outdated data
# 9. FIXED: Enabled TLS for all connections including replication with mutual TLS authentication
# 10. FIXED: Set replica-read-only to "yes" to prevent writes on replicas
# 11. FIXED: Restricted TLS to v1.2 and v1.3 only
# 12. NOTE: In production, passwords should be loaded from a secrets manager, not stored in config files
